resposta:
solution:
```
class Solution:
    def maxDepthAfterSplit(self, seq: str) -> List[int]:
        res = []
        depth = 0
        for c in seq:
            if c == '(':
                res.append(depth % 2)
                depth += 1
            else:
                depth -= 1
                res.append(depth % 2)
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution iterates through the input string once, using a single variable to keep track of the current depth. It appends the current depth modulo 2 to the result list for each character, effectively splitting the string into two valid parentheses subsequences with minimal maximum depth.
